use std::sync::Arc;
use tokio::sync::Mutex;

pub async fn refresh(cache: Arc<Mutex<Option<String>>>) -> String {
    {
        let guard = cache.lock().await;
        if let Some(value) = &*guard {
            return value.clone();
        }
    }

    let loaded = load_value().await;
    let mut guard = cache.lock().await;
    if let Some(value) = &*guard {
        return value.clone();
    }
    *guard = Some(loaded.clone());
    loaded
}

async fn load_value() -> String {
    "loaded".to_string()
}
</file>

pub fn moving_average(values: &[u32], window: usize) -> Vec<u32> {
    if window == 0 || window > values.len() {
        return Vec::new();
    }

    values
        .windows(window)
        .map(|slice| slice.iter().sum::<u32>() / window as u32)
        .collect()
}

pub struct UserId(u64);

impl UserId {
    pub fn new(value: u64) -> Self {
        Self(value)
    }

    pub fn get(self) -> u64 {
        self.0
    }
}

/// Parse an integer-like user identifier.
///
/// ```
/// use demo::{parse_user_id, UserId};
///
/// let id = parse_user_id("42").unwrap();
/// assert_eq!(id.get(), 42);
/// ```
pub fn parse_user_id(input: &str) -> Result<UserId, std::num::ParseIntError> {
    input.parse::<u64>().map(UserId::new)
}
</file>

pub fn init_process_env() {
    // SAFETY: This helper is only called during single-threaded startup before any
    // threads are spawned, so process-wide environment mutation is not concurrent.
    unsafe {
        std::env::set_var("APP_MODE", "test");
        std::env::remove_var("LEGACY_MODE");
    }
}
</file>

completion:
<file path="src/lib.rs">
#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum CacheControlError {
    InvalidDirective,
    InvalidNumber,
}

pub fn parse_max_age(input: &str) -> Result<u64, CacheControlError> {
    let trimmed = input.trim();
    let (name, value) = trimmed
        .split_once('=')
        .ok_or(CacheControlError::InvalidDirective)?;
    if name.trim() != "max-age" {
        return Err(CacheControlError::InvalidDirective);
    }
    value
        .trim()
        .parse::<u64>()
        .map_err(|_| CacheControlError::InvalidNumber)
}
</file>

<file path="src/lib.rs">
use std::ffi::CStr;
use std::os::raw::c_char;

pub fn from_c_name(name: *const c_char) -> Result<String, std::str::Utf8Error> {
    // SAFETY: The caller must provide a valid non-null pointer to a NUL-terminated C string.
    // The function reads the string but does not take ownership of the underlying memory.
    let c_str = unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(name) };
    Ok(c_str.to_str()?.to_string())
}
</file>

use std::ffi::CStr;
use std::os::raw::c_char;

pub fn from_c_name(name: *const c_char) -> Result<String, std::str::Utf8Error> {
